meaning: adj.含火绒(或朽木)的,松软的,有弹性的,闷烧的
examples: Get the lightest, punkiest wood you can find (when its punky, its not much use for models). Easy to carve and sand to shape.
Most economists, after all, see a long L-shaped recovery--two years or more of recession or punky growth.
The key is to catch the wood after the magic has begun, but before it gets too punky.
It involved the designers knowing almost everything about the game's dismal steam-punky setting.
